title: adversarial-project: context subcommand
goal: Add a 'mini context adversarial-project' subcommand that prints the same initial prompt via the shared buildProjectAdversarialContext builder (from phase 163) and accepts the same range flags (--from-phase/--to-phase and --from/--to) as the interactive command, so the future /mini:adversarial-project slash command has something to call. Exits cleanly with a non-zero code and a clear message when the builder returns null (invalid range).

steps.title: Register adversarial-project as a context command
steps.detail: Add 'adversarial-project' to CONTEXT_COMMANDS in context.ts so isContextCommand accepts it and mini context lists it; update the command's description string accordingly.

steps.title: Thread RangeInput through context()
steps.detail: Extend context(cmd, extraArgs, range?) to take an optional RangeInput; add an else-if (cmd === 'adversarial-project') branch that calls the shared buildProjectAdversarialContext(cwd, range ?? {}). The existing prompt === null -> exitCode 1 path covers the invalid-range case (builder logs the reason to stderr; only the prompt goes to stdout).

steps.title: Wire range flags onto the context command in cli.ts
steps.detail: Add --from-phase/--to-phase (via parsePhaseNumber) and --from/--to options to the context command; in the action build a RangeInput from the opts and pass it as the third arg to context(). Update the command description to mention the range flags apply to adversarial-project.

steps.title: Unit tests for the context subcommand
steps.detail: Cover: valid --from-phase/--to-phase prints the same prompt as buildProjectAdversarialContext (stdout only); invalid/empty range -> empty stdout + non-zero process.exitCode + reason on stderr; mixing phase and ref flags is rejected by the builder. Mirror existing adversarial-project / context tests.

steps.title: Update docs + mini --help surface
steps.detail: Note mini context adversarial-project in docs/ where the other context subcommands / adversarial-project are documented, so the slash-path command (6/7) has a documented contract. CHANGELOG via /mini:done.
